title: deps-guard configuration
description: Configuration file for deps-guard — blocks outdated dependencies and vulnerabilities before running scripts.

$schema.description: Points to the JSON Schema that describes this file.

critical.description: Packages that trigger exit code 1 when outdated, blocking the pipeline.

items.description: Package name as it appears in package.json (e.g. "react", "@types/node").

ignore.description: Packages to silently skip — never shown in the report, never trigger a failure.

failOn.description: Controls when deps-guard exits with code 1 due to outdated packages.

x-enumDescriptions.critical: Exit 1 only when a package listed in 'critical' is outdated.
x-enumDescriptions.any: Exit 1 when any outdated package is found (excluding ignored ones).
x-enumDescriptions.never: Always exit 0. Useful when you want the report without blocking the pipeline.

updateType.description: Minimum semver change type to include in the report. Packages below this threshold are silently ignored.

x-enumDescriptions.patch: Report all outdated packages (1.0.0 → 1.0.1 and above). Default.
x-enumDescriptions.minor: Report minor and major updates only — skip patches.
x-enumDescriptions.major: Report major updates only (1.0.0 → 2.0.0 and above).

audit.description: Enables vulnerability scanning via the package manager's audit command (npm audit, pnpm audit, etc.). Requires a network connection. Disabled by default to avoid slowing down every predev run.

auditLevel.description: Minimum vulnerability severity to include in the report. Vulnerabilities below this level are silently ignored.

x-enumDescriptions.high: Report high and critical only. Default.

ignoreAdvisories.description: Advisory IDs to silently skip in the vulnerability report. Accepts GHSA IDs (e.g. GHSA-xxxx-yyyy-zzzz) and CVE IDs (e.g. CVE-2023-1234). Comparison is case-insensitive.

auditFailOn.description: Controls when deps-guard exits with code 1 due to vulnerabilities found by audit. Independent of failOn (for outdated packages).

x-enumDescriptions.info: Exit 1 for any vulnerability at any severity.
x-enumDescriptions.low: Exit 1 for low severity and above.
x-enumDescriptions.moderate: Exit 1 for moderate severity and above.
x-enumDescriptions.high: Exit 1 for high and critical vulnerabilities.
x-enumDescriptions.critical: Exit 1 only for critical vulnerabilities. Default.
x-enumDescriptions.never: Never exit 1 due to vulnerabilities — report only.
